blocks.arabic: اعْلَمْ رَحِمَكَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ يَجِبُ عَلَيْنَا تَعَلُّمُ أَرْبَعِ مَسَائِلَ. الْأُولَى: الْعِلْمُ؛ وَهُوَ مَعْرِفَةُ اللَّهِ، وَمَعْرِفَةُ نَبِيِّهِ، وَمَعْرِفَةُ دِينِ الْإِسْلَامِ بِالْأَدِلَّةِ.
blocks.english: Know, may Allah have mercy on you, that it is obligatory upon us to learn four matters. The first: Knowledge; which is knowing Allah, knowing His Prophet, and knowing the religion of Islam with its evidences.

footnotes.text: i.e., knowledge of Allah's names, attributes, and actions

blocks.arabic: الثَّانِيَةُ: الْعَمَلُ بِهِ. الثَّالِثَةُ: الدَّعْوَةُ إِلَيْهِ. الرَّابِعَةُ: الصَّبْرُ عَلَى الْأَذَى فِيهِ.
blocks.english: The second: Acting upon it. The third: Calling to it. The fourth: Patience with the harm encountered in its path.

blocks.arabic: وَالدَّلِيلُ قَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَالْعَصْرِ ۝ إِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ ۝ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ﴾
blocks.english: And the proof is the saying of the Most High: "By time, indeed mankind is in loss, except for those who have believed and done righteous deeds and advised each other to truth and advised each other to patience." [Sūrah al-ʿAṣr]

chapters.titleEn: Chapter: The Attributes of Allah, the Most High

blocks.arabic: وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ﴾ [سُورَةُ الشُّورَى، آيَة ١١]
blocks.english: And His saying, the Most High: "There is nothing like unto Him, and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Seeing." [Sūrah al-Shūrā, verse 11]

footnotes.text: This verse affirms both tanzīh (transcendence) and ithbāt (affirmation)

blocks.arabic: وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلِلَّهِ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلَىٰ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ﴾ [سُورَةُ النَّحْلِ، آيَة ٦٠]
blocks.english: And the saying of the Most High: "And for Allah is the highest description. And He is the Exalted in Might, the Wise." [Sūrah al-Naḥl, verse 60]

blocks.arabic: وَمِنَ الْإِيمَانِ بِاللَّهِ: الْإِيمَانُ بِمَلَائِكَتِهِ، وَهُمْ عِبَادٌ مُكْرَمُونَ، لَا يَعْصُونَ اللَّهَ مَا أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ مَا يُؤْمَرُونَ.
blocks.english: And from belief in Allah is belief in His angels. They are honored servants who do not disobey Allah in what He commands them and they do what they are commanded.

hadith.label: Hadith & Muṣṭalaḥ
hadith.title: Foundational Texts in Hadith & Terminology
hadith.description: Prophetic narrations, sciences of hadith classification

content.arabic:
أَبْدَأُ بِالْحَمْدِ مُصَلِّيًا عَلَى
مُحَمَّدٍ خَيْرِ نَبِيٍّ أُرْسِلَا
content.english:
I begin with praise, sending blessings upon
Muhammad, the best of Prophets ever sent.

content.arabic:
وَذِي مِنْ أَقْسَامِ الْحَدِيثِ عِدَّةٌ
وَكُلُّ وَاحِدٍ أَتَى وَحَدَّهُ
content.english:
And this poem comprises a number of hadith categories,
And each one comes with its definition.

content.arabic:
أَوَّلُهَا الصَّحِيحُ وَهُوَ مَا اتَّصَلْ
إِسْنَادُهُ وَلَمْ يَشُذَّ أَوْ يُعَلّ
content.english:
The first of them is the Ṣaḥīḥ (Authentic), and it is that whose
Chain is continuous, not anomalous nor defective.

content.arabic:
يَرْوِيهِ عَدْلٌ ضَابِطٌ عَنْ مِثْلِهِ
مُعْتَمَدٌ فِي ضَبْطِهِ وَنَقْلِهِ
content.english:
Narrated by an upright, precise transmitter from one like him,
Reliable in his precision and transmission.

content.arabic:
وَالْحَسَنُ الْمَعْرُوفُ طُرْقًا وَغَدَتْ
رِجَالُهُ لَا كَالصَّحِيحِ اشْتُهِرَتْ
content.english:
And the Ḥasan (Good) is that whose paths are well-known,
Though its narrators are not as renowned as those of the Ṣaḥīḥ.

texts.nameAr: نُخْبَةُ الْفِكَرِ فِي مُصْطَلَحِ أَهْلِ الْأَثَر
texts.nameEn: Nukhbat al-Fikar fī Muṣṭalaḥ Ahl al-Athar
texts.author: Ibn Ḥajar al-ʿAsqalānī

blocks.arabic: أَمَّا بَعْدُ، فَإِنَّ التَّصَانِيفَ فِي اصْطِلَاحِ أَهْلِ الْحَدِيثِ قَدْ كَثُرَتْ، وَبُسِطَتْ وَاخْتُصِرَتْ، فَسَأَلَنِي بَعْضُ الْإِخْوَانِ أَنْ أُلَخِّصَ لَهُمُ الْمُهِمَّ مِنْ ذَلِكَ.
blocks.english: To proceed: The works on the terminology of the people of hadith have become numerous, both expanded and abridged. Some of the brothers asked me to summarize for them what is important from that.

blocks.arabic: فَأَجَبْتُهُ إِلَى سُؤَالِهِ رَجَاءَ الِانْدِرَاجِ فِي تِلْكَ الْمَسَالِكِ.
blocks.english: So I responded to his request, hoping to be included among those who have traversed those paths.

footnotes.text: i.e., the paths of the hadith scholars

fiqh.label: Fiqh & Uṣūl
fiqh.title: Foundational Texts in Jurisprudence & Legal Theory
fiqh.description: Islamic law, legal maxims, and principles of jurisprudence

blocks.arabic: هَذِهِ وَرَقَاتٌ تَشْتَمِلُ عَلَى مَعْرِفَةِ فُصُولٍ مِنْ أُصُولِ الْفِقْهِ. وَذَلِكَ مُؤَلَّفٌ مِنْ جُزْأَيْنِ مُفْرَدَيْنِ: أَحَدُهُمَا الْأُصُولُ، وَالْآخَرُ الْفِقْهُ.
blocks.english: These are papers encompassing knowledge of chapters from the principles of jurisprudence. This discipline is composed of two distinct parts: one is the principles (uṣūl), and the other is the jurisprudence (fiqh).

footnotes.text: Uṣūl: the foundational principles; Fiqh: the derived rulings

blocks.arabic: فَالْأَصْلُ: مَا يَبْنَى عَلَيْهِ غَيْرُهُ. وَالْفَرْعُ: مَا يُبْنَى عَلَى غَيْرِهِ.
blocks.english: The foundation (aṣl) is that upon which something else is built. The branch (farʿ) is that which is built upon something else.

blocks.arabic: الْكَلَامُ: هُوَ اللَّفْظُ الْمُرَكَّبُ الْمُفِيدُ بِالْوَضْعِ. وَأَقْسَامُهُ ثَلَاثَةٌ: اسْمٌ، وَفِعْلٌ، وَحَرْفٌ جَاءَ لِمَعْنًى.
blocks.english: Speech: It is the compounded utterance that conveys a complete meaning according to convention. Its parts are three: a noun (ism), a verb (fiʿl), and a particle (ḥarf) that conveys meaning.

footnotes.text: By convention (bi-l-waḍʿ) meaning: according to the established usage of the Arabs

blocks.arabic: فَالِاسْمُ يُعْرَفُ بِالْخَفْضِ، وَالتَّنْوِينِ، وَدُخُولِ الْأَلِفِ وَاللَّامِ، وَحُرُوفِ الْخَفْضِ.
blocks.english: The noun is known by: the genitive case (khafḍ), nunation (tanwīn), the entrance of alif-lām, and the particles of khafḍ.
